How to make sure your app works and you're not hacked

When I was learning how to code, one of the things that confused me the most was the difference between frontend and backend. I knew the stereotype of frontend people working on Macs in cafes while backend engineers wouldn't shower and looked down upon the fake job of being a frontend engineer.

While the stereotypes are fun, now that a ton of people are vibe coding full-stack apps, it's extremely important you understand the difference to make sure you're secure and providing a good experience.

How we built a synthetic health data generator
The engineering challenges we faced trying to make health data accesible for research

When we first set out to build Endpoint our goal was to make health data available for pharmaceutical research.

There were three types of problems we had to deal with:

- Regulatory
- Technological
- Cultural

This blog focuses on how we solved the technological ones.

We had to find a way to create synthetic replicas of datasets without ever seeing the data.

Our app had to run fully on-premise, adapt to different data sources and make sense of it all, autonomously.

This was an incredibly hard task for a bunch of engineers used to build dumb webapps. Here's a few of the things we noticed immediately.
